title: Accenture’s climate leadership strategy comprises four pillars. Match each pillar with the appropriate description.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text:
Accenture’s climate leadership strategy comprises four pillars. Match each pillar with the appropriate description.


1.
Leading by example

 
Involving suppliers in action on climate change

2.
Magnifying impact

 
Partnering with clients and ecosystem partners

3.
Innovating

 
Working with employees to ensure a greener future

4.
Empowering eco-action

 
Setting science-based, industry-leading Net Zero and other ambitious sustainability goals

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text:
1. Leading by example - Setting science-based, industry-leading Net Zero and other ambitious sustainability goals 
2. Magnifying impact - Partnering with clients and ecosystem partners 
3. Innovating - Working with employees to ensure a greener future 
4. Empowering eco-action - Involving suppliers in action on climate change

turns.speaker: user
turns.text:
The UN refers to 2020–2030 as the “decade of action” to tackle climate-related challenges. What is the primary factor behind the damage caused to the Earth’s natural warming systems?


Ecosystem extinction

Permafrost thawing

Industrial Revolution

Ocean acidification

Global warming

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text: The primary factor behind the damage caused to the Earth’s natural warming systems is Global warming.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text:
How are investors and insurers pressurizing organizations to act on climate change?


By pushing suppliers and other value chain partners to provide cleaner products and services

By prompting demand shifts toward more responsible products, services, and brands

By framing regulations and policies to force companies into action

By adjusting their portfolios toward companies and products that integrate environmental, social, and governance (ESG) factors into their operations and decision-making processes

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text: Investors and insurers are pressurizing organizations to act on climate change by adjusting their portfolios toward companies and products that integrate environmental, social, and governance (ESG) factors into their operations and decision-making processes. This sends a signal to companies that they need to prioritize sustainability and responsible practices if they want to attract investment and insurance coverage.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text:
Why must businesses embrace breakthrough technology, such as artificial intelligence (AI) and green cloud, to take action on climate change?


Technology can help businesses quickly transition to renewable sources of energy.

Technology can help businesses not only reduce their carbon emissions but also their cost of operations.

Technology can help businesses make their value chains agile in a volatile market.

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text:
All of the given options are correct. 

Breakthrough technology, such as artificial intelligence (AI) and green cloud, can help businesses take action on climate change by:

- Helping businesses quickly transition to renewable sources of energy by optimizing energy use and identifying opportunities for renewable energy deployment.
- Helping businesses not only reduce their carbon emissions but also their cost of operations by identifying inefficiencies and finding more sustainable solutions.
- Helping businesses make their value chains agile in a volatile market by providing real-time data and insights, allowing businesses to make informed decisions that minimize their environmental impact.
